pattern: Only the vowel run touching the very end of the string matters, so a single backward scan that stops at the first trailing consonant is enough — no need to inspect or remove vowels anywhere else in the string.
hints: Look only at the tail of the string — a vowel buried in the middle should never be removed, only a run of vowels that reaches all the way to the last character.
Walk backward from the last character, counting how many consecutive characters are vowels before you hit a consonant (or run out of string).
Once you find the first consonant scanning from the end (or reach the start), slice the string up to that point — everything before it is kept untouched, everything after it is dropped.

edge_inputs.description: single vowel — the whole string is trimmed away
all vowels — result is the empty string
vowel run in the middle followed by a consonant then a single trailing vowel — only the final 'a' should be trimmed, not the whole run
leading vowels, consonant in the middle, vowel run at the end — a solution that strips ALL vowels (not just the trailing run) fails this
max length (100), only the last char is a vowel run after a lone leading consonant
max length (100), string ends in a consonant so nothing is trimmed
already-example shape, no trailing vowels at all

stress.description: random lowercase strings of varying length, seeded, covering a mix of vowel/consonant tail shapes
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, size)
    letters = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
